theory_content:
😌 How to Manage Stress

Stress is your body's reaction to challenges. Learning to manage stress helps you feel better, think clearer, and enjoy life more.

🔍 Identify Your Stress Triggers

The first step is knowing what causes your stress. Common triggers include school deadlines, social pressure, and too many responsibilities.

🌬️ Quick Relaxation Techniques

Deep Breathing (4-7-8): Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 7, exhale for 8. Repeat 4-5 times.

🏃‍♀️ Long-Term Stress Management

  • Exercise regularly — 30 minutes of movement releases endorphins
  • Get enough sleep — teens need 8-10 hours
  • Eat healthy foods — reduce caffeine and sugar
  • Connect with others — talk to someone you trust
  • Set boundaries — learn to say no

questions.text: What is the first step in managing stress?
questions.explanation: Knowing what causes your stress helps you find the right solutions.

questions.text: How long should you hold your breath in the 4-7-8 breathing technique?
questions.explanation: Breathe in for 4 seconds, hold for 7 seconds, exhale for 8 seconds.

questions.text: What hormone does exercise release that improves mood?
questions.explanation: Endorphins are natural mood boosters released during exercise.

questions.text: How much sleep do teenagers typically need?
questions.explanation: Teens need 8-10 hours of sleep for proper health.

questions.text: Which is a healthy way to cope with stress?
options: Spending all day on social media
questions.explanation: Connecting with others provides support and reduces stress.

theory_content:
💰 How to Create a Budget

A budget is a plan for your money. It helps you spend less than you earn and save for things you want.

📝 Step 1: Calculate Your Income

Income is money you receive. For teens: allowance, part-time job, gift money.

📊 Step 2: Track Your Expenses

Track everything: food, phone bill, transportation, entertainment.

📐 Step 3: Use the 50/30/20 Rule

  • 50% for NEEDS — essential expenses
  • 30% for WANTS — non-essential fun stuff
  • 20% for SAVINGS — money for future goals

🎯 Step 4: Set Savings Goals

Be specific: 'I will save $50 per month for 6 months to buy a new phone.'

questions.text: What is a budget?
questions.explanation: A budget is simply a plan that helps you track income and expenses.

questions.text: According to the 50/30/20 rule, what percentage should go to savings?
questions.explanation: 20% of your income should go to savings.

questions.text: What does 'pay yourself first' mean?
options: Put savings away immediately when you get money
Spend all your money on yourself
questions.explanation: Pay yourself first means setting aside savings before spending on anything else.

questions.text: Which is an example of a NEED?
questions.explanation: A phone bill is a need. Wants are things like games and luxury items.

questions.text: What is the first step in creating a budget?
questions.explanation: First, know how much money you have coming in each month.

theory_content:
🧼 Kitchen Safety & Hygiene

Cooking is fun, but kitchens can be dangerous. Learning safety rules protects you and anyone you cook for.

🧽 Personal Hygiene First

  • Wash hands with soap for 20 seconds before cooking
  • Tie back long hair
  • Roll up loose sleeves
  • Remove jewelry that could fall into food

🔪 Knife Safety

  • Always cut away from your body
  • Use a stable cutting board
  • Keep knives sharp — dull knives slip
  • Never leave knives in a sink full of soapy water

🔥 Stove and Oven Safety

  • Turn pot handles inward
  • Use oven mitts for hot pans
  • Never leave cooking food unattended

If a pan catches fire: NEVER use water. Slide a lid over the pan to smother the fire.

🍗 Food Safety & Cross-Contamination

  • Use separate cutting boards for raw meat and produce
  • Cook meat to safe internal temperatures (chicken: 165°F)
  • Refrigerate leftovers within 2 hours

questions.text: What should you do if a grease fire starts in a pan?
options: Slide a lid over the pan
questions.explanation: Slide a lid over the pan to smother the fire. NEVER use water on a grease fire.

questions.text: How long should you wash your hands before cooking?
questions.explanation: Wash hands with soap for 20 seconds.

questions.text: Which way should pot handles face on the stove?
options: Toward the back of the stove
questions.explanation: Turn handles inward so no one bumps them.

questions.text: Why shouldn't you put knives in a sink full of soapy water?
options: Someone could reach in and get cut
questions.explanation: Hidden knives in soapy water are dangerous.

questions.text: What is cross-contamination?
options: Bacteria spreading from one food to another
questions.explanation: Cross-contamination is when bacteria from raw meat spreads to ready-to-eat foods.

theory_content:
🧹 How to Clean Your Room

A clean room helps you think clearly, sleep better, and feel more in control. Plus, it's easier to find things!

🎵 Step 1: Set the Mood

  • Play upbeat music (set a timer for 15-20 minutes)
  • Open windows for fresh air
  • Gather supplies: trash bag, laundry basket, cleaning spray

🗑️ Step 2: Quick Toss (5 minutes)

  • Empty food wrappers and drink bottles
  • Old papers and junk mail
  • Broken items you can't fix

👕 Step 3: Gather Laundry

Put ALL dirty clothes in a laundry basket. Check under the bed, in the closet, and behind furniture.

📦 Step 4: Put Things in Their Home (10 minutes)

  • Books on shelf
  • School supplies in desk
  • Games in designated spot

🧺 Step 5: Make Your Bed

A made bed instantly makes your whole room look cleaner. It takes only 2 minutes!

🧽 Step 6: Dust and Wipe Surfaces

🪣 Step 7: Vacuum or Sweep

Vacuum carpets or sweep hard floors. Don't forget under the bed.

questions.text: What's the first step when cleaning your room?
questions.explanation: Always start by throwing away trash.

questions.text: How often should you do a full room cleaning?
questions.explanation: A weekly full cleaning (15-20 minutes) keeps your room tidy.

questions.text: What should you do with dirty clothes?
options: Put them in a laundry basket
questions.explanation: Put dirty clothes in a laundry basket.

questions.text: How does a clean room affect your mental state?
questions.explanation: A clean room reduces visual clutter, which helps reduce mental clutter.

questions.text: What's a good daily cleaning habit?
questions.explanation: Making your bed daily takes 2 minutes and makes your room look cleaner.

theory_content:
💰 How to Save Money as a Teen

Saving money as a teenager sets you up for financial success as an adult. Start now — even small amounts add up!

💵 Ways to Earn Money

  • Babysitting or pet sitting
  • Lawn mowing or yard work
  • Tutoring younger students
  • Part-time job at local stores
  • Selling handmade items online

🏦 Open a Savings Account

Ask a parent to help you open a youth savings account. Your money earns interest and is safe.

🎯 Set SMART Savings Goals

Specific: I want a new phone. Measurable: $300. Achievable: I can save $30/month. Relevant: I really want it. Time-bound: By December.

questions.text: What is a good way to earn money as a teen?
questions.explanation: Babysitting, tutoring, and part-time jobs are great ways to earn.

questions.text: Why open a savings account?
options: To keep money safe and earn interest
questions.explanation: A savings account keeps your money safe and pays you interest.

questions.text: What is a SMART goal?
options: I will save $50 per month for 6 months to buy a new phone
questions.explanation: SMART goals are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound.

questions.text: What should you do before buying something expensive?
questions.explanation: Waiting 24 hours helps you avoid impulse purchases.

questions.text: What is a want?
questions.explanation: Wants are non-essential things like games, eating out, and new clothes.

theory_content:
🥚 How to Boil an Egg

Boiled eggs are perfect for breakfast, salads, or quick snacks. Learn the foolproof method for perfect eggs every time.

📋 Steps

  1. Place eggs in a single layer in a pot
  2. Add enough cold water to cover eggs by 1 inch
  3. Bring water to a rolling boil over high heat
  4. Turn off heat, cover pot, and let sit
  5. For soft-boiled: 4-6 minutes
  6. For hard-boiled: 10-12 minutes
  7. Transfer eggs to ice water bath to stop cooking
  8. Peel and enjoy!

questions.text: How long for hard-boiled eggs after turning off heat?
questions.explanation: Hard-boiled eggs need 10-12 minutes in hot water.

questions.text: Why put eggs in ice water after boiling?
options: To stop cooking and make peeling easier
questions.explanation: Ice water stops the cooking process and helps the shell separate.

questions.text: How much water should cover the eggs?
questions.explanation: Add enough cold water to cover eggs by about 1 inch.

questions.text: What do you do after bringing water to a boil?
options: Turn off heat and cover pot
questions.explanation: Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and let eggs sit in hot water.

questions.text: Soft-boiled eggs sit for how long?
questions.explanation: Soft-boiled eggs need 4-6 minutes for runny yolks.

theory_content:
🧼 How to Wash Dishes Properly

Knowing how to wash dishes is a basic life skill. Learn the right way to get dishes truly clean.

📋 Step-by-Step Method

Step 1: Scrape Off Food

Scrape leftover food into the trash. Don't rinse it down the sink.

Step 2: Fill Sink with Hot Water

Add dish soap. Hot water kills germs and cuts through grease.

Step 3: Sort and Wash in Order

Wash cleanest to dirtiest: glasses, silverware, plates, pots.

Step 4: Scrub Thoroughly

Scrub all surfaces — front, back, and edges.

Step 5: Rinse with Hot Water

Rinse off soap under hot running water.

Step 6: Dry Properly

Air dry on a dish rack, or dry immediately with a clean towel.

questions.text: What should you do FIRST before washing dishes?
questions.explanation: First, scrape leftover food into the trash.

questions.text: What is the correct order for washing dishes?
options: Glasses first, then silverware, then plates, then pots
questions.explanation: Wash cleanest items (glasses) first, then silverware, plates, and finally greasy pots.

questions.text: What water temperature is best for washing dishes?
questions.explanation: Hot water cuts through grease and kills more germs.

questions.text: Why shouldn't you stack dirty dishes?
questions.explanation: Stacking dirty dishes means you have to clean both sides.

questions.text: How do you remove stuck food from a pan?
options: Soak in hot soapy water first
questions.explanation: Soaking loosens stuck food and makes scrubbing much easier.

theory_content:
💪 How to Build Self-Esteem

Self-esteem is how you see and value yourself. When you have healthy self-esteem, you feel good about who you are and believe in your ability to handle life's challenges.

🧠 Signs of Healthy Self-Esteem

  • You accept yourself, flaws and all
  • You can laugh at your mistakes
  • You handle criticism without falling apart
  • You try new things without fear of failure
  • You set boundaries and say no when needed

⚠️ Signs of Low Self-Esteem

  • You're very hard on yourself
  • You focus on weaknesses, not strengths
  • You feel you don't deserve good things
  • You avoid challenges because you might fail
  • You seek constant approval from others

🗣️ Challenge Negative Self-Talk

Negative thought: I'm so stupid, I failed that test.

Balanced thought: I didn't do well on this test, but that doesn't mean I'm stupid. I can study differently next time.

📝 Practice Self-Compassion

Treat yourself like you would treat a good friend. Ask yourself: What would I say to my best friend in this situation?

questions.text: What is self-esteem?
options: How you see and value yourself
questions.explanation: Self-esteem is your overall sense of self-worth as a person.

questions.text: Which is a sign of healthy self-esteem?
questions.explanation: People with healthy self-esteem accept themselves completely.

questions.text: What should you do with negative self-talk?
options: Challenge and replace it with balanced thoughts
questions.explanation: Challenge negative thoughts by questioning if they're really true.

questions.text: What is self-compassion?
options: Treating yourself like you'd treat a good friend
questions.explanation: Self-compassion means being kind and understanding toward yourself.

questions.text: Why is comparing yourself to others on social media harmful?
options: People only post their highlights, not struggles
questions.explanation: People post their best moments online, not their daily struggles.

theory_content:
🥗 How to Make a Simple Salad

Salads are healthy, quick, and customizable. Once you know the basics, you can make a salad with almost any ingredients.

🥬 Step 1: Choose Your Greens

  • Lettuce (iceberg, romaine, green leaf)
  • Spinach
  • Arugula
  • Kale
  • Mixed spring greens

🥕 Step 2: Add Vegetables

  • Tomatoes (cherry or chopped)
  • Cucumbers
  • Carrots (shredded or sliced)
  • Bell peppers
  • Red onion
  • Avocado

🍗 Step 3: Add Protein (Optional)

  • Grilled chicken
  • Hard-boiled eggs
  • Chickpeas or beans
  • Tofu
  • Tuna
  • Cheese (feta, cheddar, mozzarella)

🥑 Step 4: Add Healthy Fats

  • Avocado
  • Nuts (almonds, walnuts)
  • Seeds (sunflower, pumpkin)
  • Olives

🥣 Step 5: Make a Simple Dressing

Basic vinaigrette: 3 parts oil + 1 part vinegar + salt + pepper

🍽️ Step 6: Toss and Enjoy

Combine everything in a large bowl, add dressing, toss well, and serve immediately.

questions.text: What is the first step in making a salad?
questions.explanation: Always start with your base greens.

questions.text: What is a basic vinaigrette ratio?
questions.explanation: The classic ratio is 3 parts oil to 1 part vinegar.

questions.text: Why should you dry your greens before adding dressing?
options: Wet greens make dressing slide off
questions.explanation: Dry greens help dressing stick to the leaves.

questions.text: When should you add dressing to salad?
questions.explanation: Add dressing right before eating to prevent sogginess.

questions.text: Which is a good source of protein for a salad?
questions.explanation: Eggs, chicken, beans, and tofu are good protein sources.

theory_content:
👥 How to Deal with Peer Pressure

Peer pressure happens when friends or classmates try to influence your decisions. Learning to say no is a superpower that will serve you for life.

🚫 Types of Peer Pressure

  • Spoken: Someone directly asks you to do something
  • Unspoken: Everyone is doing it, so you feel left out
  • Direct: Come on, everyone is doing it
  • Indirect: Seeing others do something and feeling pressured to join
  • Positive: Friends encourage you to do something good
  • Negative: Friends pressure you to do something harmful or wrong

💪 How to Say No (6 Techniques)

  • Simple No: No thanks.
  • Give a Reason: No, I have to study.
  • Suggest Something Else: I don't want to do that, but let's play video games instead.
  • Walk Away: You don't have to stay.
  • Use an Excuse: I can't. I have an early morning tomorrow.
  • The Broken Record: Repeat your answer calmly. No. No. I said no.

questions.text: What is peer pressure?
options: When friends try to influence your decisions
questions.explanation: Peer pressure is when people your age try to influence what you do.

questions.text: What is an example of unspoken peer pressure?
options: Someone asks you to do something
Everyone is doing it, so you feel left out
questions.explanation: Unspoken peer pressure happens when you see others doing something and feel pressured to join.

questions.text: What is a good way to say no?
questions.explanation: A simple 'No thanks' is often enough.

questions.text: What can you do if friends won't stop pressuring you?
questions.explanation: You can always walk away. Real friends will respect your choices.

questions.text: What is positive peer pressure?
options: Friends pressure you to skip school
Friends encourage you to study for a test
questions.explanation: Positive peer pressure encourages good behavior like studying, exercising, or being kind.

theory_content:
🧀 How to Make a Grilled Cheese Sandwich

Grilled cheese is a classic comfort food. It's easy to make, but there are tricks to make it perfect.

🛒 Ingredients

  • 2 slices of bread
  • 1-2 slices of cheese
  • 1 tablespoon butter (softened)

🍳 Equipment

  • Frying pan or skillet
  • Spatula
  • Butter knife

📋 Step-by-Step

  1. Butter one side of each slice of bread.
  2. Place one slice, buttered side down, in a cold pan. Add cheese.
  3. Top with the second slice, buttered side up.
  4. Cook on medium-low heat for 2-3 minutes until golden brown.
  5. Flip carefully. Cook the other side for 2-3 minutes.
  6. Remove, cool for 1 minute, cut in half, and enjoy!

💡 Pro Tips

  • Use low to medium heat — patience prevents burning.
  • Cover the pan with a lid for the last minute to help cheese melt faster.
  • Use two types of cheese for more flavor.
  • Add extras: tomato slices, bacon, ham, or avocado.

questions.text: Where should you put the butter?
options: On the outside of both bread slices
questions.explanation: Butter goes on the outside of both slices for crispy golden crust.

questions.text: What heat setting should you use?
questions.explanation: Medium-low heat prevents burning and allows cheese to melt fully.

questions.text: How long do you cook each side?
questions.explanation: Cook each side for 2-3 minutes until golden brown.

questions.text: What can help cheese melt faster?
options: Covering the pan with a lid
questions.explanation: Covering the pan traps heat and helps cheese melt faster.

questions.text: What's a classic pairing with grilled cheese?
questions.explanation: Tomato soup is the classic pairing with grilled cheese.

theory_content:
💡 How to Change a Light Bulb

Changing a light bulb is simple, but doing it safely matters. Learn the right way for different types of bulbs.

⚠️ Safety First

  • Turn off the light switch
  • Let bulb cool down if it was on
  • Use a stable step stool or ladder — not a chair
  • Never touch a bulb with wet hands

🛠️ What You'll Need

  • New bulb (check wattage and size)
  • Step stool or small ladder

📋 Step-by-Step

  1. Turn off the light switch.
  2. Let bulb cool for 5-10 minutes.
  3. Remove old bulb: standard — turn counter-clockwise (left). Pin-type — pull straight out.
  4. Insert new bulb — screw in clockwise until snug.
  5. Test — turn the light back on.

💡 Bulb Types

  • LED: Most energy efficient, last longest
  • CFL: Energy saving, contain small amount of mercury (recycle properly)
  • Incandescent: Traditional bulbs, use more energy

♻️ Disposal

  • LED and incandescent: Regular trash
  • CFL: Take to recycling center — they contain mercury

questions.text: What should you do FIRST before changing a light bulb?
questions.explanation: Always turn off the light switch first for safety.

questions.text: How do you remove a standard screw bulb?
questions.explanation: Standard bulbs screw out counter-clockwise (left).

questions.text: Why should you let a bulb cool before touching it?
options: Bulbs get very hot and can burn you
questions.explanation: Bulbs get extremely hot when on — let them cool.

questions.text: Which bulb type is most energy efficient?
questions.explanation: LED bulbs use the least energy and last the longest.

questions.text: What should you NEVER do with CFL bulbs?
questions.explanation: CFL bulbs contain mercury and must be recycled.

theory_content:
✍️ How to Write a Thank You Note

A handwritten thank you note shows appreciation and makes people feel valued. It's a small effort with big impact.

📝 When to Write a Thank You Note

  • After receiving a gift
  • After someone helps you
  • After a job interview
  • After staying at someone's house
  • To thank a teacher or mentor
  • Just because — to show appreciation

✉️ What You'll Need

  • Blank card or paper
  • Pen
  • Envelope

📋 The 4-Part Formula

  • Greeting: Dear Name,
  • Say Thank You and Be Specific: Thank you so much for the art set. I love the watercolors!
  • Share How You'll Use It: I've already used it to paint a picture of our dog.
  • Close Warmly: Sincerely, With love, Best regards,

questions.text: What is the first part of a thank you note?
questions.explanation: Start with a greeting like 'Dear Grandma,'.

questions.text: Why should you be specific in a thank you note?
questions.explanation: Specific thanks show you really noticed and appreciated the gift or help.

questions.text: How soon should you send a thank you note?
questions.explanation: Send thank you notes within 2 weeks for best impact.

questions.text: How should a thank you note end?
options: With a warm closing like 'Sincerely' or 'Love'
questions.explanation: End with a warm closing followed by your name.

questions.text: Which is better for a thank you note?
questions.explanation: Handwritten notes feel more personal and thoughtful.

theory_content:
🎒 How to Pack a School Bag

A well-packed backpack helps you stay organized, find things quickly, and avoid back pain. Learn the right way to pack.

🎒 Choose the Right Backpack

  • Two padded shoulder straps
  • Padded back for comfort
  • Multiple compartments for organization
  • Right size — not too big, not too small
  • Lightweight material

📋 What to Pack Daily

  • Textbooks needed for the day
  • Notebooks and folders
  • Pencil case
  • Laptop or tablet (if needed)
  • Water bottle
  • Lunch or snack

📦 How to Pack — The Layer Method

  • Layer 1 (Bottom): Heavy Items — textbooks at the bottom, closest to your back
  • Layer 2 (Middle): Medium Items — binders, notebooks, folders
  • Layer 3 (Top): Light Items — lunch, jacket, light items
  • Small Compartments: Pencil case, calculator, phone, keys, wallet

questions.text: Where should heavy items go in your backpack?
options: At the bottom, closest to your back
questions.explanation: Heavy items should be at the bottom, closest to your back for better weight distribution.

questions.text: What percentage of your body weight should your backpack be?
questions.explanation: Backpack should weigh no more than 10-15% of your body weight.

questions.text: Why should you use both shoulder straps?
options: Distributes weight evenly and prevents back pain
Both straps are required by law
questions.explanation: Using both straps distributes weight evenly and prevents back strain.

questions.text: When should you pack your backpack for school?
questions.explanation: Packing the night before saves morning stress.

questions.text: What should you do weekly with your backpack?
options: Clean it out and check for damage
questions.explanation: Weekly cleaning removes old papers and lets you check for damage.

theory_content:
🍜 How to Make Instant Noodles Better

Instant noodles are cheap and fast, but they can be boring. With a few simple additions, you can turn them into a real meal.

🥚 Easy Add-Ins

  • Egg: Crack an egg into the noodles during the last minute of cooking
  • Frozen vegetables: Peas, corn, carrots
  • Green onions: Chop and sprinkle on top
  • Soy sauce: A splash adds depth
  • Sesame oil: A few drops for nutty flavor
  • Hot sauce: Sriracha, chili oil

🍳 Perfect Egg in Noodles

  1. Cook noodles according to package
  2. During the last minute, crack an egg directly into the pot
  3. Don't stir — let egg poach for 1-2 minutes
  4. Serve with runny yolk

🍲 Broth Upgrades

  • Use half the seasoning packet (less sodium)
  • Add a spoonful of peanut butter for creamy Thai-style broth
  • Splash of milk or coconut milk for creamy broth
  • Rice vinegar or lime juice for tang

questions.text: When should you add an egg to instant noodles?
options: During the last minute of cooking
questions.explanation: Add egg during the last minute of cooking so it poaches perfectly.

questions.text: What happens if you overcook instant noodles?
questions.explanation: Overcooked noodles become mushy.

questions.text: What can you add for creamy Thai-style broth?
questions.explanation: A spoonful of peanut butter makes creamy, Thai-style broth.

questions.text: What can you add for tangy flavor?
questions.explanation: Lime juice or rice vinegar adds bright, tangy flavor.

questions.text: What can you do with leftover noodles?
questions.explanation: Leftover noodles can be stir-fried with vegetables and sauce for a new meal.

theory_content:
👟 How to Tie Your Shoes (Different Methods)

Most people learn one way to tie shoes. But there are multiple methods — some faster, some stronger, some easier for kids. Learn them all!

🎀 Method 1: Standard Bunny Ears

  1. Cross the laces and pull tight
  2. Make a loop with each lace
  3. Cross the loops
  4. Pull one loop through the hole
  5. Pull both loops tight

⚡ Method 2: Standard Knot (Fastest)

  1. Cross the laces and pull tight
  2. Make a loop with one lace
  3. Wrap the other lace around the loop
  4. Pull the second lace through the hole
  5. Pull both loops tight

🔒 Method 3: Double Knot (Stays Tied Longest)

  1. Tie a standard knot
  2. Tie another knot on top
  3. Pull tight

questions.text: Which method is best for young kids learning to tie shoes?
questions.explanation: The bunny ears method is easiest for kids.

questions.text: Which method keeps shoes tied longest?
questions.explanation: Double knotting makes shoes much less likely to come untied.

questions.text: What type of laces stay tied better?
questions.explanation: Flat laces stay tied better than round laces.

questions.text: When should you replace shoelaces?
questions.explanation: Replace laces when they become frayed to prevent breaking.

questions.text: What is the first step in tying shoes?
questions.explanation: First, cross the laces and pull tight.

theory_content:
👋 How to Introduce Yourself

Introducing yourself can feel awkward, but it's a skill anyone can learn. A good introduction makes a positive first impression and starts conversations.

📝 The Formula for Any Introduction

Hello + Name + Something About You + Question

Example: Hi, I'm Alex. I'm in Mr. Chen's biology class. What class are you in?

🎯 For Different Situations

  • School / New Class: Hi, I'm [Name]. I just moved here. What's your name?
  • Job Interview: Hello, I'm [Name]. It's nice to meet you. I've been working on [skill] for the past two years.
  • Party / Social Event: Hey, I'm [Name]. How do you know the host?

💡 Body Language Tips

  • Make eye contact
  • Smile naturally
  • Stand up straight
  • Offer a handshake (in professional settings)
  • Speak clearly

questions.text: What is the basic formula for an introduction?
options: Hello + Name + Something about you + Question
questions.explanation: Hello + Name + Something about you + Question starts the conversation.

questions.text: What should you do after someone tells you their name?
options: Say it back: 'Nice to meet you, Sarah'
questions.explanation: Repeating their name helps you remember it.

questions.text: Which is good body language during an introduction?
questions.explanation: Eye contact and smiling show confidence.

questions.text: What should you do if you're nervous?
options: Take a deep breath before speaking
questions.explanation: Deep breathing calms nerves before an introduction.

questions.text: What's a good opening for a new student?
options: Hi, I'm new here. What's your name?
questions.explanation: A friendly, simple opening works best when you're new.

theory_content:
⏰ How to Read a Clock

Knowing how to read a clock is an essential life skill. While phones show digital time, analog clocks are everywhere — schools, offices, train stations.

🕰️ Parts of an Analog Clock

  • Hour hand (short hand): Tells the hour
  • Minute hand (long hand): Tells the minutes
  • Second hand (thin hand): Tells seconds
  • Numbers 1-12: Mark the hours
  • Tick marks: Small lines between numbers for minutes

📖 How to Read the Hour

The hour hand is the short hand. If it points exactly at 3 → it's 3 o'clock. If it's between 3 and 4 → it's still 3 something.

🔢 How to Read the Minutes

Each number represents 5 minutes: 12=0, 1=5, 2=10, 3=15, 4=20, 5=25, 6=30, 7=35, 8=40, 9=45, 10=50, 11=55.

📝 Examples

  • Short hand at 3, long hand at 12 → 3:00
  • Short hand at 3, long hand at 6 → 3:30
  • Short hand at 3, long hand at 9 → 3:45

questions.text: Which hand tells the hour?
questions.explanation: The short hand tells the hour.

questions.text: What time is it when the short hand is at 2 and long hand at 12?
questions.explanation: Long hand at 12 means :00. Short hand at 2 means 2:00.

questions.text: What number does the minute hand point to for 30 minutes?
questions.explanation: 6 × 5 = 30 minutes.

options: AM is morning, PM is afternoon/evening
AM is afternoon, PM is morning
AM is digital, PM is analog

theory_content:
🧹 How to Use a Broom and Dustpan

Sweeping seems simple, but doing it right makes cleaning faster and more effective. Learn proper technique.

🛠️ What You'll Need

  • Broom
  • Dustpan
  • Trash can

📋 Step-by-Step

  1. Clear the floor of large items
  2. Start at one corner of the room
  3. Sweep in long, even strokes toward you
  4. Work from corners toward the center
  5. Sweep dirt into a pile in one spot
  6. Position dustpan flat on floor
  7. Sweep dirt into dustpan
  8. Empty dustpan into trash

💡 Pro Tips

  • Sweep toward you, not away
  • Use short strokes for corners
  • Dampen broom slightly to catch fine dust
  • Sweep in one direction, not back and forth

questions.text: Where should you start sweeping?
questions.explanation: Start at a corner and work toward the center.

questions.text: Which direction should you sweep?
questions.explanation: Sweep toward you in long, even strokes.

questions.text: What should you do before sweeping?
questions.explanation: Clear chairs, rugs, and toys before sweeping.

questions.text: What helps catch fine dust?
questions.explanation: A slightly damp broom catches fine dust better.

questions.text: What do you do after sweeping dirt into a pile?
questions.explanation: After creating a pile, sweep it into the dustpan.

theory_content:
📞 How to Make a Phone Call

Phone calls can feel intimidating, but they're an important skill. With practice, anyone can learn to call confidently.

📝 Before You Call

  • Know why you're calling
  • Have pen and paper ready to write notes
  • Find a quiet place
  • Take a deep breath

🗣️ The Call Structure

  • Greeting: Hello, this is [Your Name].
  • State Your Purpose: I'm calling to make an appointment. or I have a question about...
  • Conversation: Listen and respond. Ask questions if needed.
  • Closing: Thank you. Have a good day. Goodbye.

📱 Example Scripts

  • Calling a friend: Hey Sarah, it's Alex. What are you doing Saturday?
  • Calling a doctor's office: Hello, this is Taylor Jones. I need to make an appointment.
  • Calling to order food: Hi, I'd like to place an order for pickup.

questions.text: What should you do before making a call?
options: Write down what you want to say
questions.explanation: Writing notes helps you remember what to say.

questions.text: What's a good greeting for a phone call?
questions.explanation: A clear greeting identifies who you are.

questions.text: What helps nervous callers feel more confident?
questions.explanation: Practice builds confidence.

questions.text: What should you have ready during a call?
questions.explanation: Pen and paper let you write down important information.

questions.text: What's a good closing for a phone call?
questions.explanation: A polite closing ends the call professionally.

fields.title: How to Do Laundry
fields.description: Learn to sort, wash, dry, and fold clothes correctly. Keep your clothes looking good and lasting longer.
fields.theory_content:
🧺 How to Do Laundry

Doing laundry is an essential life skill that everyone needs to learn. Once you understand the basics, it's easy and takes very little time. This lesson will teach you everything you need to know — from sorting to folding.

Laundry is easier than you think

👕 Step 1: Sort Your Laundry

Sorting is the most important step. It prevents color bleeding and ensures clothes are washed correctly.

  • Whites — white shirts, socks, underwear, towels (wash in hot water)
  • Darks — black, navy, dark gray, dark brown (wash in cold water)
  • Colors — red, blue, green, bright colors (wash in warm or cold water)
  • Delicates — lace, silk, bras, thin fabrics (use mesh bags, delicate cycle)
  • Towels & Heavy — bath towels, jeans, hoodies (wash separately, use warm water)

Why sort? Dark colors can bleed dye onto whites and ruin them forever. Different fabrics also need different washing settings to avoid damage.

🏷️ Step 2: Check Care Labels

Every clothing item has a tag with care symbols. This is your guide to proper washing. Look for:

  • 🌡️ Temperature — cold, warm, hot (shown as dots in a water bucket)
  • 🌀 Cycle — normal, delicate, hand wash (shown as lines under the bucket)
  • ❌ Do Not Wash — dry clean only (circle symbol)
  • 🧴 Bleach — triangle means safe, triangle with X means no bleach
  • 🌀 Wring — twisted symbol means can be wrung, X means don't wring

If the label says "Dry Clean Only" — don't put it in the washing machine! Take it to a professional.

🧴 Step 3: Add Detergent

Detergent cleans your clothes. There are different types, and the right amount matters.

  • Liquid detergent: Pour into the measuring cap. Use the lines on the cap — one or two lines for a normal load. Pour into the detergent drawer or directly into the drum.
  • Powder detergent: Use the scoop inside the box. Usually 1-2 tablespoons for a normal load. Put in the drum before clothes.
  • Detergent pods: Use ONE pod per load. Place it in the drum BEFORE adding clothes — never in the drawer.
  • How much is too much? Extra detergent doesn't clean better — it leaves residue on clothes, can irritate skin, and damages the machine.

Where to put it: Most machines have a drawer. If not, put detergent directly in the drum before adding clothes.

👕 Step 4: Load the Machine

How you load matters for cleaning quality and machine health.

  • ✅ Fill loosely — about 3/4 full. You should be able to fit your hand flat on top of the clothes.
  • ✅ Clothes need space to move around in the water and detergent to get clean.
  • ✅ Put small items (socks, underwear) in a mesh laundry bag so they don't get lost.
  • ✅ Turn jeans and dark clothes inside out to protect the color from fading.
  • ✅ Zip up zippers and button buttons to prevent snags on other clothes.
  • ✅ Empty ALL pockets — tissue paper will ruin everything and leave white flakes on all clothes!

Don't overload: If you can't fit your hand flat on top, the machine is too full and clothes won't get clean.

⚙️ Step 5: Choose Settings

Different cycles do different things. Here's what each one means:

  • 🌀 Normal / Cotton — everyday clothes, t-shirts, jeans, towels, sheets. Uses warm water and stronger agitation.
  • 🌸 Delicate / Hand Wash — silk, lace, thin fabrics, bras. Uses cold water and gentle movement.
  • ⚡ Quick Wash (30 min) — lightly soiled clothes that aren't very dirty. Uses less water and time.
  • ❄️ Cold Wash — dark colors, prevents shrinking, saves energy. Best for most everyday clothes.
  • 🔥 Hot Wash (60°C+) — towels, bed sheets, underwear, heavily soiled items. Kills bacteria but can shrink clothes.
  • 🌀 Rinse & Spin — just rinses and spins out water. Use if you forgot to add detergent.

Quick guide:

  • 👕 Everyday clothes → Normal cycle, cold water
  • 🛏️ Sheets and towels → Normal cycle, warm/hot water
  • 👖 Jeans → Normal cycle, cold water, inside out
  • 🌸 Delicate fabrics → Delicate cycle, cold water

🌬️ Step 6: Drying

Proper drying prevents wrinkles, shrinking, and mildew.

  • Machine drying: Use low or medium heat for most clothes — high heat shrinks things! Remove clothes promptly to prevent wrinkles. Clean the lint filter EVERY time — it prevents fires!
  • Air drying (better for clothes): Hang on a drying rack or clothesline. Air-dry delicate items, jeans, and anything with elastic. Drying racks save energy and make clothes last longer.
  • Never put in dryer: Wool, silk, bras with underwire, items with prints or iron-on transfers.

📦 Step 7: Folding and Putting Away

  • Fold clothes as soon as they're dry — prevents wrinkles
  • Put items away in drawers or closet immediately
  • Match socks as you fold — pair them up
  • Hang items that wrinkle easily (dresses, button shirts, blazers)

🧼 How Often to Wash Different Items

  • 👕 T-shirts, underwear, socks — after every wear
  • 👖 Jeans — every 3-5 wears (unless visibly dirty)
  • 🧥 Sweaters — every 3-5 wears
  • 🛏️ Sheets and towels — weekly
  • 🧥 Jackets — once a season or when dirty
  • 👙 Bras — every 3-4 wears

🧴 Stain Removal Quick Guide

  • 🍅 Food stains: Rinse with cold water immediately, then treat with stain remover
  • 🌿 Grass: Rub with liquid detergent, wash in cold water
  • 🩸 Blood: COLD water only (hot sets the stain), rub with soap
  • 💄 Makeup: Rub with shaving cream or makeup remover, then wash
  • 🖊️ Ink: Rub with rubbing alcohol or hand sanitizer before washing

Never put a stained item in the dryer until the stain is gone — heat sets stains permanently!

🧹 Cleaning Your Washing Machine

Your machine needs cleaning too! Do this every 1-3 months:

  1. Run an empty cycle with HOT water
  2. Add 2 cups of white vinegar OR a washing machine cleaner tablet
  3. After the cycle, wipe inside the drum and rubber seal with a dry cloth
  4. Leave the door open to air dry

🎓 Quiz Time!

Test your knowledge about laundry. You need 70% to pass and earn your points!

questions.text: Why should you sort laundry before washing?
options: Dark colors can bleed onto white clothes
questions.explanation: Dark colors can transfer dye to light clothes, ruining them.

questions.text: What temperature is best for dark clothes?
questions.explanation: Cold water prevents fading and color bleeding.

questions.text: How full should you fill the washing machine?
questions.explanation: Clothes need space to move around in the water to get clean.

questions.text: What should you always clean before drying?
questions.explanation: Clean the lint filter before EVERY load — it prevents fires and helps clothes dry faster.

questions.text: What does 'Dry Clean Only' mean?
options: Don't put in washing machine — take to dry cleaner
questions.explanation: 'Dry Clean Only' means the item cannot be machine washed — take it to a professional dry cleaner.

questions.text: What should you do with a stain BEFORE putting it in the dryer?
options: Make sure the stain is completely gone
questions.explanation: Heat from the dryer sets stains permanently. Only dry when the stain is gone.

questions.text: Where should you put laundry pods?
options: In the drum BEFORE adding clothes
questions.explanation: Pods go directly in the drum before you add clothes so they dissolve properly.

questions.text: How often should you wash sheets and towels?
questions.explanation: Sheets and towels collect bacteria and body oils — wash them weekly.

questions.text: How often should you clean your washing machine?
questions.explanation: Run an empty hot cycle with vinegar every 1-3 months to remove buildup and odors.

questions.text: What should you do with small items like socks?
options: Put them in a mesh laundry bag
questions.explanation: Small items can get lost or stuck. A mesh bag keeps them together and prevents them from slipping between the drum and door.

fields.title: How to Set a Table
fields.description: Learn proper table setting for everyday meals and special occasions. Impress your family and guests.
fields.theory_content:
🍽️ How to Set a Table

Knowing how to set a table is a valuable skill for family dinners, holidays, and when guests come over. A properly set table shows care and makes everyone feel welcome.

A set table makes meals feel special

🍽️ Everyday Setting (What You Use Most)

  • Plate: In the center of the place setting
  • Fork: Left side of plate
  • Knife: Right side of plate (blade facing the plate)
  • Spoon: Right side next to knife
  • Drink: Above the knife (right side)
  • Napkin: Left side of forks OR on top of the plate

Fork left, knife and spoon right

🎄 Formal Setting (For Holidays and Special Occasions)

  • Dinner plate: Center
  • Salad plate: Above dinner plate
  • Bread plate: Above forks (top left)
  • Butter knife: On the bread plate
  • Forks: Left side — dinner fork closest to plate, salad fork outside, fish fork farthest (if used)
  • Knives: Right side — dinner knife closest to plate, then fish knife, then steak knife (if used)
  • Spoons: Right side next to knives — soup spoon outside
  • Drink glasses: Above knives — water glass closest, then wine glass, then other drinks
  • Napkin: On the dinner plate OR folded to the left of forks

💡 Memory Tricks

  • FORK and LEFT both have 4 letters → fork goes on the left
  • KNIFE and RIGHT both have 5 letters → knife goes on the right
  • SPOON goes next to knife on the right
  • Drinks on the RIGHT — your right hand holds the drink

📝 Utensil Order Rule

Use utensils from the OUTSIDE IN. The outermost fork or spoon is for the first course. Work your way toward the plate as courses progress.

💡 Pro Tips

  • ✅ Knife blade always faces the plate
  • ✅ Napkin goes on the lap when sitting down
  • ✅ Water glass sits above the knife
  • ✅ Leave about 1 inch between plate and utensils
  • ✅ All place settings should match — same distance from plate edge
  • ✅ Forks and knives should be aligned at the bottom edge
  • ✅ Spoons are placed with the bowl facing up

🎓 Quiz Time!

Test your knowledge about table setting. You need 70% to pass and earn your points!

questions.text: Where does the fork go in an everyday table setting?
questions.explanation: Fork goes on the left side of the plate.

questions.text: What's a memory trick to remember fork placement?
options: Fork has 4 letters, LEFT has 4 letters
questions.explanation: Both FORK and LEFT have 4 letters — fork goes on left.

questions.text: Which way does the knife blade face?
questions.explanation: Knife blade always faces the plate.

questions.text: In a formal setting, what goes above the forks?
questions.explanation: Bread plate goes above the forks (top left).

questions.text: What is the rule for using utensils in a formal setting?
questions.explanation: Use utensils from the outside in — outermost for first course.

fields.title: How to Make a Bed
fields.description: Learn to make your bed quickly and neatly. A simple habit that improves your whole day.
fields.theory_content:
🛏️ How to Make a Bed

Making your bed every morning takes less than 2 minutes but makes your whole room look better and starts your day with a small win. Admiral William McRaven said: "If you make your bed every morning, you will have accomplished the first task of the day. It will give you a small sense of pride, and it will encourage you to do another task."

A made bed changes the whole room

📋 What You'll Need

  • Fitted sheet
  • Flat sheet
  • Blanket or comforter
  • Pillows with pillowcases

📝 Step-by-Step Instructions

Step 1: Remove Old Sheets

Take off all old sheets and pillowcases. Put them in the laundry basket.

Step 2: Put on Fitted Sheet

The fitted sheet has elastic corners. Stretch it over the mattress corners one by one. Make sure it's snug and smooth.

Step 3: Put on Flat Sheet

Lay the flat sheet over the bed. The top edge should align with the top of the mattress. The pattern side faces DOWN so the pattern shows when folded over.

Step 4: Make Hospital Corners

This technique keeps sheets tight and neat:

  1. Tuck the bottom edge of the sheet under the mattress at the foot of the bed
  2. Lift the side edge of the sheet about 12 inches from the corner
  3. Fold it into a triangle
  4. Tuck the hanging part under the mattress
  5. Fold the triangle down and tuck it under the mattress
  6. Repeat on both sides

Hospital corners keep sheets tight

Step 5: Add Blanket

Lay the blanket on top of the flat sheet. Fold the top edge of both the blanket and flat sheet down about 6 inches.

Step 6: Fluff and Place Pillows

Put pillowcases on pillows. Fluff them by holding them on opposite ends and pushing them together. Place them at the head of the bed.

Step 7: Add Decorative Touches (Optional)

Add throw pillows, a folded blanket at the foot, or a bed runner for extra style.

⚡ The 60-Second Version

In a rush? Just pull up your comforter and fluff your pillows. Still looks 80% better!

💡 Pro Tips

  • ✅ Make bed as soon as you get up — before you forget
  • ✅ Keep extra pillowcases in your nightstand for quick changes
  • ✅ Wash sheets weekly to keep them fresh
  • ✅ Rotate your mattress every 6 months for even wear
  • ✅ Use a mattress protector to prevent stains and extend mattress life

🎓 Quiz Time!

Test your knowledge about making a bed. You need 70% to pass and earn your points!

questions.text: What is the first step in making a bed?
questions.explanation: Always remove old sheets first and put them in the laundry.

questions.explanation: The fitted sheet has elastic corners that stretch over the mattress.

options: Corners that go to the hospital
A folding technique that keeps sheets tight
questions.explanation: Hospital corners are a folding technique that keeps sheets tight and neat.

questions.text: How often should you wash sheets?
questions.explanation: Wash sheets weekly to remove sweat, oil, and dust mites.
